mechanism_narrative: A sustained high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a tight monetary policy stance by the central bank, which typically translates into higher long-term interest rates and thus an upward direction for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield.
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield does not show a positive direction in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and weakening economic conditions, prompting investors to seek safety in U.S. Treasuries, which drives bond yields down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates an environment where the Federal Reserve is expected to maintain or increase interest rates, pushing long-term bond yields higher to curb inflationary pressures.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3%
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield direction is negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where both GDPC1 growth and CPIAUCSL inflation exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) during a monetary tightening cycle indicates that the central bank will need to keep policy rates elevated, leading to upward pressure on long-term Treasury yields as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk.
failure_condition: During monetary tightening cycles, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a negative direction in more than 40% of instances where CPIAUCSL is above 3.5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently strong retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand and overall economic health, which typically leads to higher inflation expectations and a greater likelihood of central bank tightening, pushing bond yields higher.
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield direction is negative or flat in more than 60% of periods when RSAFS shows consistent month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic weakness, leading to expectations of lower future growth and inflation, which drives bond yields down.
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 1% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS) coupled with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a tight labor market, increasing wage pressures and inflation expectations, which prompts the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance, leading to higher bond yields.
failure_condition: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield direction is negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where both PAYEMS growth is strong and UNRATE is low.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) during a period of high interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals a significant slowdown in the housing market, which can spill over into broader economic weakness, leading to lower bond yields as investors anticipate future rate cuts.
failure_condition: During high interest rate environments, the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield shows a positive direction in more than 40% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month.
